title: Carrot Soufflé
description: Light, sweet, and perfectly spiced baked carrot soufflé. Somewhere between a dessert and a side dish — irresistibly good.

ingredients.note: peeled and sliced, approx. 4–5 medium carrots

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 1.5-quart baking dish.
Peel, slice, and boil carrots for 15–20 minutes until very tender — they should mash easily with a fork.
Drain carrots thoroughly and allow steam to escape for 2–3 minutes.
Transfer carrots to blender and puree on high until completely smooth.
Add melted butter, eggs, sugar, flour, baking powder, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt to the blender.
Blend again for 30–45 seconds until fully combined and smooth.
Pour mixture into the greased baking dish.
Bake for 45–50 minutes until set in the center (a toothpick should come out clean).
Allow to cool for 10 minutes before serving. Serve warm.

notes: This soufflé walks the line between a sweet side dish and a dessert — perfect alongside Thanksgiving turkey or as a stand-alone treat.
